headlines.title: Mike Pence says GOP ‘lost our way’ after nominating Ken Paxton ‘but Democrats have lost their mind’
headlines.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence tells Meet the Press Democrats have “lost their mind” and Republicans can still hold onto their Senate majority, but says the party has “lost our way” after Texas Republicans nominated Ken Paxton for Senate.

headlines.title: Trump suggests canceling performances at ‘Great American State Fair’ after artists back out
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday suggested canceling planned musical performances at the Great American State Fair in Washington this summer after several artists pulled out of the event.“We should have a giant MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN RALLY, for 250, instead of having overpriced singers, who nobody wants to hear, whose music is boring, and yet who do nothing but complain,” Trump said in a post on Truth Social Saturday afternoon.“Cancel it,” Trump said, before launching into a separate

headlines.title: Netanyahu vows to expand Israel’s grip on Lebanon after deepest incursion in 26 years
headlines.description: Israeli forces have crossed the Litani River and captured a strategic mountain in Lebanon, marking the deepest incursion in 26 years.

headlines.title: Spurs Beat Reigning Champion Thunder to Face Knicks in Finals
headlines.description: The NBA Finals are now set after the San Antonio Spurs won a dramatic Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals against reigning champions, the Oklahoma City Thunder. The Spurs, who are just a couple of years removed from being the among the worst ranked teams in the league, will host the New York Knicks in Game 1 of the finals.

headlines.title: The book fueling a movement against screens in schools
headlines.description: “The Digital Delusion” by Jared Cooney Horvath connects the collapse in standardized test scores to the rise of education technology in public schools.

headlines.title: Protests Outside Newark ICE Detention Center Enters 10th Day
headlines.description: Protesters and police have been clashing outside of an immigration center in Newark, New Jersey, for more than a week. Newark’s mayor implemented a curfew for the area that will be in effect from 9 p.m. to 6 a.m. going forward after several people were arrested on Saturday. NBC’s Valerie Castro reports for Sunday TODAY.

headlines.title: Bus driver in Virginia crash that killed 5, including family, charged with manslaughter
headlines.description: The driver of a bus in a crash that killed five people in Virginia on Friday, including a family of four en route to a relative’s wedding, has been charged with involuntary manslaughter

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Claude Lemieux’s brain is being donated to Boston University’s CTE Center, family says
headlines.description: Claude Lemieux’s brain is being donated to the Boston University CTE Center to research the long-term effects of repetitive brain injuries, his family said Saturday in a statement released by daughter Claudia Lemieux Bishop

headlines.title: Mike Pence says the Trump administration has ‘departed’ from conservative principles
headlines.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence said the second administration of President Donald Trump has “departed” from traditional conservative principle in the president’s second term.

headlines.title: After new drug’s ‘unprecedented’ results for pancreatic cancer, doctors look at other uses
headlines.description: The experimental drug daraxonrasib, which doubled survival time in patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, may also prove effective for lung, colon and ovarian cancers.

headlines.title: U.S. faces foreign crises with a hollowed out diplomatic corps
headlines.description: Hundreds of diplomats have been forced to retire or were laid off in the Trump administration
